content_markdown: A fixed mass of an ideal gas is trapped in a cylinder of constant volume and its temperature is varied. Which graph shows the variation of the pressure of the gas with temperature in degrees Celsius?

diagram_metadata.description: Four graphs showing pressure on the y-axis and temperature in degrees Celsius on the x-axis. Graph A shows a linear increase starting from a non-zero pressure at 0 degrees Celsius. Graph B shows a decreasing curve. Graph C shows a linear increase starting from the origin. Graph D shows a linear decrease.

vibe_explanation: According to the Ideal Gas Law $PV = nRT$, pressure is directly proportional to absolute temperature (Kelvin). Since $T(K) = T(°C) + 273.15$, the relationship between pressure and Celsius temperature is linear ($P \propto T_{Celsius} + 273.15$), which means the graph is a straight line with a positive y-intercept at $0°C$. Graph C is incorrect because pressure is only zero at absolute zero ($-273.15°C$), not at $0°C$.
